records.ety: [OE. wolle, wulle, AS. wull; akin to D. wol, OHG. wolla, G. wolle, Icel. & Sw. ull, Dan. uld, Goth, wulla, Lith. vilna, Russ. volna, L. vellus, Skr. ūrā wool, v to cover. √146, 287. Cf. Flannel, Velvet.]

defs: The soft and curled, or crisped, species of hair which grows on sheep and some other animals, and which in fineness sometimes approaches to fur; -- chiefly applied to the fleecy coat of the sheep, which constitutes a most essential material of clothing in all cold and temperate climates.
Short, thick hair, especially when crisped or curled.
quotes: Wool of bat and tongue of dog.

defs: A sort of pubescence, or a clothing of dense, curling hairs on the surface of certain plants.
